description_long: This is a vintage manual coffee grinder with a square wooden base and a metal grinding mechanism mounted on top. The body is constructed from light-colored wood, featuring dovetail joints at the corners and a small drawer at the front for collecting ground material. The top houses a round, slightly domed metal chamber with a hand-crank handle extending outward, ending in a rounded knob for easy turning. The handle is made of metal and is slightly curved, showing signs of wear and age. The front of the grinder has a small wooden knob attached to the drawer, which can be pulled out to access the ground contents. The overall appearance is rustic and utilitarian, with visible scratches and patina indicating frequent use.
description: A vintage manual coffee grinder with a wooden box base, metal grinding mechanism, and a hand-crank handle. It features a pull-out drawer for collecting grounds and a rustic, well-used appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wooden drawer with a knob, dovetail joints, and the metal grinding mechanism with the handle on top.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the curved metal handle, the round metal grinding chamber, and the plain wooden side with a visible screw.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain wooden back, the metal top, and the handle extending to the left.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the handle from a different angle, the wooden side, and the side of the drawer knob.
attribute_handle_design: The handle is a single, curved metal rod with a rounded knob at the end, positioned horizontally on top of the grinder. It lacks ergonomic features and appears simple and functional.
